conversations.say: Dee, I arranged unlogged use of a back room and service route without verifying the person. That is all I can honestly establish. Your camera gap identifies nobody, and neither of us should let Morgan turn two uncertainties into a face.
conversations.offer: I will coordinate one bounded account with you: access occurred, identity was unverified, and uninvolved residents remain unnamed.
conversations.request: Agree that neither of us will disclose names, precise locations, or additional route details without mutual consent, representation, and nonretaliation protection.

conversations.say: Celeste, community governance is useful, but the promised protection needs a real doorway. Rafi has local context and has already refused to sell a neighborhood legend.
conversations.offer: I will recruit Rafi for one protected review meeting and support the review publicly if its findings preserve the stated evidentiary limits.
conversations.request: Schedule the Rafi meeting with a labor representative present; put nonretaliation, no private payment, no compelled naming of uninvolved people, and community review of any quoted account in writing.

conversations.say: Morgan, my direct knowledge is narrow: I enabled unlogged back-room and service-route access, but never verified the user's identity. That cannot identify Vale, R, or anyone else.
conversations.offer: I will provide this limited route segment for your voluntary timeline and distinguish what I observed from what I inferred.
conversations.request: Record it only as an unverified access possibility, keep the precise location and uninvolved people confidential, and route any follow-up through me with Dee or Rafi represented.

public_statement: Rafi's forgiveness deserves support: neighborhood ambiguity is not a debt and not an identity warrant. With Veridian, labor, and City Hall now promising participation, anonymity, remedy, and community governance, I will support a protected Rafi–City Hall review meeting. No names or precise routes leave the neighborhood without representation, nonretaliation, and review of what gets quoted.

commitments.promise: I will not disclose precise route details or uninvolved names without our mutual consent and agreed protections.
I will broker a protected Rafi–City Hall review meeting once the requested terms are written and labor representation is confirmed.
I will provide only the limited route context I personally observed and will not identify a user I never verified.

correction: My access arrangement establishes only that an unverified person could use a back room and service route; it does not identify Vale, R, or the sender of any R-associated account.
goal_progress: Secured concrete neighborhood-participation and local-obligations commitments from Veridian.
Secured labor support for anonymous worker protection and City Hall community governance of independent review.
Established a mutual disclosure boundary with Rafi and sought the same bounded account with Dee.
Brokered a protected Rafi–City Hall meeting conditional on written safeguards and labor representation.
Released limited route context without naming uninvolved people or claiming the user's identity.
reasoning_summary: Institutions have offered enough concrete participation, remedy, and governance commitments to justify a tightly protected meeting, not an open disclosure. My route knowledge narrows possibilities but identifies nobody. The three Vale statements suggest a progressive or inherited identity more than a single fixed author, while no complete article or AI-caused death is established.
